text: Skin diseases as a global public health priority. This is a short page. The Seventy-eighth World Health Assembly recognized skin diseases as a global public health priority.

The Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 identified 4.69 billion incident cases of skin diseases. Acknowledging that most of the skin diseases burden in any community is caused by about 10 common general skin diagnoses.
To consider innovative integrated service delivery models, including telemedicine platforms and training for digital assessments. Short.
To develop a results-based and capabilities-driven global plan of action on public health responses to skin diseases. Filler.
To report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Eightieth World Health Assembly in 2027. End.
